2605.27311.title: Chartographer: Counterfactual Chart Generation for Evaluating Vision-Language Models
2605.27311.paper_summary: Chart question-answering (QA) benchmarks aim to pose questions that require visual reasoning to correctly answer, but models can often reach solutions through shortcuts or prior familiarity with a chart based on their own background knowledge. To strictly evaluate visual reasoning, we propose counterfactual charts where the chart-question task remains fixed, but underlying chart and the corresponding answer are varied. We introduce Chartographer, a framework to reverse engineer charts into executable code, validate reconstruction fidelity, generate seed-controlled counterfactual variants, and derive new answers from executable QA logic. We apply this framework to existing chart QA datasets and evaluate proprietary and open-source vision-language models (VLMs), measuring variation sensitivity and generalizability. Counterfactual charts reveal failures hidden by single-chart performance: VLMs often fail to generalize after answering the original chart correctly. We find failures are most prevalent when updated charts require novel visual reasoning pathways.
2605.27311.paper_summary_zh: 圖表問答（QA）基準旨在提出需要視覺推理才能正確回答的問題，但模型通常可以通過捷徑或基於自身背景知識的先前熟悉度來達成解決方案。為了嚴格評估視覺推理，我們提出反事實圖表，其中圖表-問題任務保持不變，但基礎圖表和相應的答案則有所變化。我們介紹了Chartographer，一個將圖表逆向工程為可執行代碼的框架，驗證重建的忠實度，生成種子控制的反事實變體，並從可執行的QA邏輯中導出新答案。我們將這個框架應用於現有的圖表QA數據集，並評估專有和開源的視覺-語言模型（VLMs），測量變異敏感性和可泛化性。反事實圖表揭示了單一圖表性能隱藏的失敗：VLMs在正確回答原始圖表後，通常無法泛化。我們發現當更新的圖表需要新的視覺推理途徑時，失敗最為普遍。
2605.27311.author: Yifan Jiang et.al.
2605.27311.authors: Yifan Jiang, Dae Yon Hwang, Jesse C. Cresswell, Freda Shi
